message:
feat(ui_query): tick 197 list_pane_bookmarks_with_cx + list_saved_searches_with_cx cx-threaded (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Both wrappers were pre-flight-only: they did one cx.checkpoint()
then delegated to ambient-cx legacy. The docstring on
list_pane_bookmarks_with_cx even admitted:

  "StorageHandle has no _with_cx siblings yet (147 async fns without
  cx surface). The pre-flight checkpoint is still valuable for UI
  surfaces..."

That comment was written before tick 149 landed the 140/140 storage
pub-async-fn sibling milestone. Both siblings exist now:

- storage.list_pane_bookmarks_with_cx(cx)  (storage.rs L7232)
- storage.list_saved_searches_with_cx(cx)  (storage.rs L7101)

Tick 197 routes through the cx-first storage siblings. Legacy
`list_pane_bookmarks` / `list_saved_searches` preserved.

Under a caller-cancelled cx, the storage read pre-flight checkpoint
fires — no wasted DB work or result materialization.

Verification: 31 / 31 ui_query:: tests pass under
--features asupersync-runtime via fork bypass, including
`list_pane_bookmarks_with_cx_matches_legacy_on_empty_db` (the
contract pin between legacy and cx-first variants).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch: @@ -104,23 +104,25 @@ pub async fn list_pane_bookmarks(storage: &StorageHandle) -> crate::Result crate::Result> { cx.checkpoint() .map_err(|err| crate::Error::Runtime(format!("list_pane_bookmarks cancelled: {err}")))?; - list_pane_bookmarks(storage).await + let records = storage.list_pane_bookmarks_with_cx(cx).await?; + Ok(records.into_iter().map(PaneBookmarkView::from).collect()) } /// List saved searches for UI surfaces. @@ -129,16 +131,21 @@ pub async fn list_saved_searches(storage: &StorageHandle) -> crate::Result crate::Result> { cx.checkpoint() .map_err(|err| crate::Error::Runtime(format!("list_saved_searches cancelled: {err}")))?; - list_saved_searches(storage).await + let records = storage.list_saved_searches_with_cx(cx).await?; + Ok(records.into_iter().map(SavedSearchView::from).collect()) } /// Resolve ruleset profile status, including the currently active profile.
